rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 7, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff Christopher Muse¿s motion to appoint Christopher Muse as successor-in-interest to Patricia Ann Neal, and substituting him in her capacity as successor-in-interest to Patricia Ann Neal, deceased, as plaintiff, and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAY D HOLLIDAY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Severing Defendant Gibbs & Cox, Inc. From Case
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 7, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Plaintiffs¿ motion for order severing defendant Gibbs & Cox from this case is denied. While the court is sympathetic to the rationale for the motion, severing a single defendant and staying the action as to that defendant pending the decision on appeal in another case raises a host of possible adverse case management issues given the uncertainty of when the court of appeal will issue a decision on the Baragar appeal and the possible running of the 5 year statute. The hearing on this motion will be at 9:00 a.m., not 9:30 a.m. = (220/HEK)
